sentences.text: Hey, how are you?
Hi, good, how are you?
Doing well.
You know, have you had a good week so far?
Yes, it's been busy.
How about you?
Same.
I feel like I have been going non-stop since Monday morning.
Yeah.
That's crazy.
Yeah.
How's, how's things on your end?
Busy, they're good.
Yeah, I wanted to connect on the contract piece, because I'll probably have some changes from what was originally sent over.
And then I wanted to clarify, because I had a couple of our team members who were interested in signing up for the remainder of the year.
Okay.
But then… I just wanted to get an understanding for 2026, because what if they don't re-sign in 2026?
Because our approvals, basically, we won't get until, like, November, so I'm… I'm not 100% confident that they're all going to be able to go into 2026, if that makes sense.
Yeah.
Yeah, no, that makes sense.
Hmm.
So, the only thing is that we… we do a minimum of 6 months.
That's why I said on, like, the properties, if they decided that they were, we could redo an agreement for the 2026 time period that supersedes the initial agreement.
But for those other properties, they would basically be locked in for, like, the first 3 months.
If they didn't want to move forward for, like, all of 2026 or something.
Got it.
Okay, so I'm gonna have to… recirculate the email to the team then, because I… I just don't know who can 100% commit right now, just because our budgets aren't confirmed until November.
Right.

So, I have people who will do 3 months, and then they might stay on in 2026, but they're not going to do the 6-month necessarily, so if your team can't be flexible with that, I just can't guarantee anything.
And the number of people who come on might shrink.
And then that other issue that it sets us up with is then, if it's only 8th and Republican, you guys have a minimum of 5 properties, correct?
Correct.
8th and Republican, you're losing the business there.
Yeah, yeah, how many properties, I guess, do you foresee potentially coming on?
I have 8 confirmed, maybe 10.
Okay.
We could do through the end of this year.
And then, like, they might continue into 26, I just can't guarantee it, because our budgets don't get approved until November, so that's where the issue is.
So some of them, like, they have additional funds right now, because we're towards the end of the year, so they're able to kind of test it.
You know what I mean?
Yeah.
It's just that 6-month minimum is an issue, and then some of them maybe will be fine and can do the 6-month, I just can't, like… guarantee any of them until November.
Right, right, right.
No, I get that.
So, you're saying some may be able to do the 6 months, but some cannot.
I… but I won't know until November.
They all end up doing the six months.
It just depends on how their… if their budget's approved.
Because what happens is we go through budget review, and then they might say, you're over budget in marketing, so now they gotta cut costs, and so where are they going to look to cut costs?
Are they cost, you know, cutting rentable?
Are they cutting the Zillow?
Are they… you know what I mean?
Yeah, no, and that makes total sense.
So 8 to 10… 10-year may not be able…
Okay.
Because I can take it to my team and see what their thoughts are.
I haven't seen them approve anything lower than 6 months and over the past year, but if you're bringing on 10 properties to start, they may be able to.
Do you know of any properties that would for sure be doing 6 months, and be able to do through the beginning?
Like, 8th and Republican, obviously?
Yeah, that one, and then I can send an email and ask for others.
Okay.
And I just am not confident about knowing until… Yeah, November.
Gotcha, okay.
Okay, let me ask my team.
Recirculate the email, I guess, and just see how many would be able to do 6 months.
As well, and then we can kinda… figure out for sure where to go.
I just don't want to promise you that, and then my team turn around and be like, oh no, like, we won't approve that.
But I have… if you get up to 10 properties, I have an idea that they may make an exception.
Yeah.
Okay.
But I'll… I'll definitely ask.
Awesome, thank you.
And then, yeah, I'll circulate an email to the group, too, and try and get more information from them.
Cool.
Yeah.
That works.
What other questions do you have for me?
That's the only thing that I have right now, Yeah, and if we can work towards that, and then I'll send over any changes, too, to the, contract that needs to be made.
Okay, that works.
It's, like, ready to go for next week.
There is a new, like, vendor portal that we would need to get rentable signed up in and all of that, so I should be able to send details on that over, too.
like a vendor shield kind of thing.
You guys would probably do it with other management companies as well.
Yeah, yeah, within Yardi, yeah, we definitely do, and that's not a big deal.
If you send me over the information, I can have my team make sure everything's set up on our end, and then they can… Get the appropriate stuff sent over and everything, too.
Awesome.
Do you have, Because you said 8, and then maybe 10.
Do you have the names of those properties?
Yeah, I can send that over in an email.
Cool, that'll work.
That'll just allow me to go ahead and start putting them in in the agreement and whatnot, too.
But it will need to be two separate agreements.
for 3 months… oh, yeah, yeah, for 8th and Republican versus the other 8.
Yes.
Okay.
Cool.
Yeah, I can definitely do that.
That's not a big deal at all.
I've already actually have it set up in there where I have two separate agreements going for the different properties, so…
Okay, awesome.
Thank you.
Yeah, no problem.
So I guess I'll let you send that, and I'll go and talk to my VP as soon as we hop off the call.
And then… Trying to think of any other questions that he may ask.
Because you won't have budgets finalized for 2026 until November.
Yes.
Okay.
So then, like, we could sign again in November for…
For 20…
what he said.
Right.
Okay.
That…
That shouldn't be an issue.
Okay.
Yeah, I'm just trying to think of any questions he'll ask.
I can't think of them off the top of my head of what he would ask.
for… Since we typically do 6 months, or, like, any wording that he'd offer putting in there.
The biggest concern is, like, just not knowing if the budget will be there for 2026, even though it's there right now.
Right.
Okay.
Okay, cool.
I can, I can take that to him for sure and see what he says.
Awesome, yeah, and then keep me posted.
I'll send over the list of properties, and then I'll also recirculate to the group to see if anyone can do a 6-month.
Okay.
Maybe our 6-month would just be fewer properties, but yeah, we'll kind of figure that out.

Yeah, and even if… and I guess, like, the thing I could also see him saying is, like, well, if you can get to the 5 guaranteed 6 months, and the other 5 potentially can only do 3 months, like… Doing it to where we let those do a 3-month, since there's at least a 5 on a 6.
But I can… I can talk to him, and I can let you know as well.
Okay.
Alright.
Thank you so much.
Yeah, you're welcome.
I, I look forward to working together, though.
I think we'll figure something out one way or the other.
Yeah, I appreciate it.
Yeah, no problem.
I guess in terms of a follow-up with each other, when… when do we want to follow up from here?
Today's Wednesday, Thursday, I'm out of the office on Friday, working just, Limited access to stuff, so maybe Monday?
Okay.
Because then we can try and nail down, like, the Eastern Republican thing.
Yeah, for sure.
And then what's the turnaround time, say, when… by the time you get the contract back?
How long from then until it's, like, live?
Typically within, like, a week to a week and a half.
Okay.
So, it's a relatively quick turnaround process.
The only thing that we, get from you is we make sure that all the information is correct for your properties, and then they walk you through how to turn on integration into Rent Cafe.
And then, from there…
They ask you for, where you want leads sent, so, like, if you need a lead tracking email or lead tracking phone numbers.
They'll ask you for those, and then we'll optimize everything, make sure it's ready to go, and… It'll be up and running.
It broke off, I'm sorry, did you say something?
Oh yeah, I just said that sounds great.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, I don't know if my internet's going out.
Somebody… somebody cut the line in my neighborhood on Sunday morning.
And it didn't get up and running until… to yester… No, sorry, Monday at 6pm, so I was out without internet for 2 days, so I think since then my internet's kind of been a little spotty.
Yeah, oh geez.
Yeah.
But cool.
We'll all get that information over to you, and then I'll talk with my VP, and then I'll let you circulate everything around, and we'll touch base again on Monday.
Awesome, thank you.
Do you wanna…
cheating.
No problem.
Do you want to do another Zoom call like what we're doing now?
Yeah.
Okay, specific time that works better?
I'm gonna do, like, same time?
Yeah, 10.
30… yeah, that works great.
Cool.
30 my time, yeah.
I will send you over an invite then.
Awesome, thank you.
Thank you, Morgan.
Have a good one, and enjoy your long weekend, or long yesterday.
Thanks.
Yeah, you too.
Have a good weekend.
Thanks.
